alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Siamese architecture reduces the identity verification Equal Error Rate (EER) to 1.29%. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: There is still much room to further improve performance of existing metal artifact reduction methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EPE-NAS can produce a robust correlation between untrained networks and their trained performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EPE-NAS can produce a robust correlation between untrained networks and their trained performance. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: EPE-NAS is agnostic to the search method, making it easy to integrate into almost any NAS method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sentiment analysis in images is a valuable complement to textual data as it enables the inference of the underlying message polarity. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a method that combines both textual and image individual sentiment analysis into a final fused classification based on AutoML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using Mathews correlation coefficient as early stopping demonstrates an average 15% dice coefficient improvement compared to the commonly used BCE.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified distance map loss further improves the segmentation performance by another 2%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning model developed can be applied to other biomedical imaging modalities such as MRI and X-ray imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning model developed can be applied to other biomedical imaging modalities such as MRI and X-ray imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same application claim with similar confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for multi-sensor change detection utilizes only unlabeled target bi-temporal images for training a network in a self-supervised manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for multisensor change detection utilizes only unlabeled target bitemporal images for training a network in a self-supervised manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core methodological contribution with essentially identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method incorporates deep clustering and contrastive learning techniques to enhance change detection performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method incorporates deep clustering and contrastive learning techniques to enhance the change detection process.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the techniques claimed (deep clustering and contrastive learning) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the proposed method on four multi-modal bi-temporal scenes demonstrates its effectiveness in detecting changes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benefits of the self-supervised approach are demonstrated through evaluation on four multimodal bitemporal scenes showing change.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same evaluation result on the same four scenes and the same conclusion about demonstrated benefits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional supervised machine learning and unsupervised generative approaches are limited by constraints such as the use of only target image-pairs and lack of labeled data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this stated limitation about traditional approaches; the preprint's critique is not present in the published claim set.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed statistical hypothesis tests for the regression function of binary classification are based on conditional kernel mean embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed statistical hypothesis tests for the regression function of binary classification are based on conditional kernel mean embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The methodological basis of the tests is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tests allow for control of the exact probability of type I error for any sample size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tests allow for control of the exact probability of type I error for any sample size.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the finite-sample type I error control claim as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both proposed techniques are consistent under weak statistical assumptions, with type II error probabilities pointwise converging to zero.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both proposed techniques are consistent under weak statistical assumptions, with type II error probabilities pointwise converging to zero.
alignments.rationale: Consistency and vanishing type II error are preserved as in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The regression function determines both the Bayes optimal classifier and the misclassification probabilities.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an added explanatory claim connecting the regression function to Bayes optimal classifier and misclassification probabilities that did not appear as an explicit claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed FCM based classifier demonstrates performance comparable to off the shelf classical methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FCM based classifier demonstrates performance comparable to off the shelf classical methods.
alignments.rationale: The performance-comparison result is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the $d$-step classifier can be attributed to its ability to transform the feature space by grouping observations belonging to a given class.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the d-step classifier can be attributed to its ability to transform the feature space by grouping observations belonging to a given class.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory hypothesis about the d-step classifier's mechanism is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Statistical analyses confirm the performance of the FCM based classifier and its capability to improve data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Statistical analyses confirmed the performance of the FCM based classifier and its capability to improve data.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the statistical analysis results essentially identically (tense change only), so the claim remains.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The FCM based classifier utilizes a fully connected map structure and executes multiple iterations before collecting output labels.
alignments.rationale: This implementation-detail claim about map structure and iterative execution appears in the publication but was not stated as a claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The one-split and two-split tests relax the assumptions and computational complexity of existing black-box tests for neural networks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The one-split and two-split tests relax the assumptions and computational complexity of existing black-box tests. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication sentence is effectively identical in meaning and scope to the preprint claim, with no substantive change in wording or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The one-split test estimates and evaluates a black-box model through sample splitting and data perturbation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The one-split test estimates and evaluates a black-box model through sample splitting and data perturbation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same technical claim with matching detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-split test requires no perturbation and further splits the inference subset into two. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-split test requires no perturbation and further splits the inference subset into two. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same description of the two-split test with no substantive alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed tests demonstrate utility on seven simulated examples and six real datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed tests demonstrate utility on seven simulated examples and six real datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning techniques have garnered considerable research attention for thwarting Android malware attacks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the domain-specific claim about Android malware research attention; instead it presents a more general statement about deep learning, so this specific claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A total of 132 studies on deep learning approaches for Android malware defenses were identified covering the period 2014-2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A total of 132 studies on deep learning approaches for Android malware defenses were identified covering the period 2014–2021. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative literature-search result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of the identified studies focus on deep learning-based Android malware detection, while 53 primary studies (40.1 percent) design defense approaches based on other scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The majority of the identified studies primarily focus on deep learning-based Android malware detection. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the primary clause that most studies focus on detection; the numeric breakdown appears separately in another publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: No systematic literature review focusing on deep learning approaches for Android malware defenses exists prior to this study. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: No systematic literature review focusing on deep learning approaches for Android malware defenses previously exists. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution/novelty claim (no prior SLR), with only a minor change in labeling (limitation vs contribution) but identical substantive content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The popular OKVQA dataset has serious limitations, including a large fraction of queries that do not assess the ability to integrate cross-modal information. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The popular OKVQA dataset has serious limitations, including a large fraction of queries that do not assess the ability to integrate cross-modal information. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same limitation claim verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Frequency-based guessing is very effective in the OKVQA dataset due to widespread answer overlaps between the train and test folds. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Frequency-based guessing is very effective in the OKVQA dataset due to widespread answer overlaps between the train and test folds. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical limitation regarding frequency-based guessing and answer overlap.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset is designed only for the quantitative assessment of the end-to-end answer retrieval task, lacking provisions for assessing the correct semantic interpretation of the input query. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset is designed for the quantitative assessment of the end-to-end answer retrieval task, with no provision for assessing the correct semantic interpretation of the input query. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same limitation about semantic interpretation assessment with equivalent emphasis and detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new dataset and challenge, based on the structural idiom S3, has been built to address the limitations of the OKVQA dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new dataset and challenge, based on the structural idiom S3, has been built to address the limitations of the OKVQA dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution of the new S3-based dataset and challenge is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural OKVQA system S3 explicitly addresses the challenge dataset and outperforms recent competitive baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The S3 neural system explicitly addresses the challenge dataset and outperforms recent competitive baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported empirical result for the S3 system is carried over with the same claim of outperforming baselines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PROVED tool helps to explore, navigate and analyze uncertain event data by abstracting the uncertain information using behavior graphs and nets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PROVED tool abstracts uncertain information using behavior graphs and nets with Petri nets semantics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core abstraction claim but adds specificity (Petri nets semantics), which clarifies implementation details while preserving the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Uncertain events, process traces and logs contain attributes characterized by quantified imprecisions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Uncertain events, process traces and logs contain attributes characterized by quantified imprecisions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same factual claim but presents it with higher certainty, indicating an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The PROVED tool enables discovery and conformance checking based on the constructs of behavior graphs and nets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool enables discovery and conformance checking based on the constructs of behavior graphs and nets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same capability claim for discovery and conformance checking with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The waveform selection problem can be effectively addressed using a linear contextual bandit formulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The waveform selection problem can be effectively addressed using a linear contextual bandit formulation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological conclusion with unchanged scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations demonstrate that the proposed formulation provides a substantial improvement in target detection performance when Thompson Sampling and EXP3 algorithms are used. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed formulation provides a substantial improvement in target detection performance when Thompson sampling and EXP3 algorithms are used. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical simulation result is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The harmful impacts of pulse-agile behavior on coherently processed radar data can be mitigated by adopting a time-varying constraint on the radar's waveform catalog. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The harmful impacts of pulse-agile behavior on coherently processed radar data can be mitigated by adopting a time-varying constraint on the radar's waveform catalog. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same mitigation conclusion but presents it with reduced certainty (high -> medium), indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biomedical imaging allows noninvasive assessment of disease based on visual evaluations leading to better clinical outcome prediction and therapeutic planning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biomedical imaging allows non-invasive assessment of diseases, leading to better clinical outcome prediction and therapeutic planning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The two statements are effectively identical in meaning and certainty; only minor wording differences (noninvasive vs non-invasive, disease(s)) exist.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrated statistical and deep learning methods have recently emerged as a new direction in the automation of the medical practice unifying multi-disciplinary knowledge in medicine, statistics, and artificial intelligence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrated statistical and deep learning methods represent a new direction in the automation of medical practice. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert the same contribution that integrated statistical and deep learning methods are a new direction in automating medical practice; the publication text is a slightly more concise restatement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results do highlight that model-driven classical statistics and data-driven deep learning is a potent combination for developing automated systems in clinical oncology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-driven classical statistics and data-driven deep learning is a potent combination for developing automated systems in clinical oncology. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result/conclusion without substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adjusted learning model takes mathematical information into account and learns the order of numerical operations more robustly. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The adjusted learning model developed in this study takes mathematical information into account and learns the order of numerical operations more robustly. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with only minor wording changes; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study achieved a 98.80% correct answer rate with symbolic integration, which is higher than any existing method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study achieved a 98.80% correct answer rate with symbolic integration, which is higher than any existing method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical numeric result and claim wording indicate no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An integrated model based on the strategy of checking the consistency of the derivative of the primitive function with the integrand achieved a 99.79% rate of correct answers with symbolic integration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By building an integrated model based on the strategy of checking the consistency of the derivative of the primitive function with the integrand, a 99.79% rate of correct answers with symbolic integration was achieved. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same methodological strategy and identical performance metric; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Transcription Style Block (TSB) can learn to switch between multiple transcription styles without explicit knowledge of transcription rules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Transcription Style Block (TSB) can learn to switch between multiple transcription styles without explicit knowledge of transcription rules. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact contribution claim with no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TSB improves text recognition accuracy on large-scale real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TSB improves text recognition accuracy on large-scale real-world data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TSB learns semantically meaningful transcription style embedding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TSB learns semantically meaningful transcription style embeddings. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses the plural form 'embeddings' but conveys the same result about semantically meaningful style representation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TSB can efficiently adapt to transcription styles of new documents from transcriptions of only a few text lines. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TSB can efficiently adapt to transcription styles of new documents from transcriptions of only a few text lines. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The adaptation capability is stated identically with matching certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average MAE of the translated synthetic CT images is 29.31 HU, significantly improved from the average MAE of 162.77 HU of the real week 1 CBCT images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average mean absolute error (MAE) of the real week 1 CBCT images is reduced from 162.77 HU to 29.31 HU after translation to synthetic CT images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the identical numeric MAE reduction and attribute it to translation to synthetic CT images; wording differences do not change meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average structural similarity of the synthetic CT images with the planning CT images is 92%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average structural similarity of the synthetic CT images with the pCT images is 92%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: pCT is equivalent to planning CT; the numeric similarity metric is identical, so no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average DICE scores for the 3D organs-at-risk segmentations are 0.96 for lungs, 0.88 for heart, 0.83 for spinal cord, and 0.66 for esophagus. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the simultaneous generation of high-quality segmentation masks for different organs-at-risk (OARs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication turns specific DICE-score results into a more general conclusion about producing high-quality OAR segmentation masks; this is a summarization/broadening of the specific quantitative results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach could allow clinicians to adjust treatment plans using only the routine low-quality CBCT images, potentially improving patient outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This method could enable clinicians to adjust treatment plans using only routine low-quality CBCT images, potentially improving patient outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about clinical utility is restated with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method generates a visual attention map using bio-inspired representation learning that combines low-level contrast and high-level semantic features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed visual attention prediction method effectively combines low-level contrast and high-level semantic features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the effective combination of features; it omits the explicit phrase 'generates a visual attention map' but preserves the core contribution, making the wording slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes a densely connected block to concatenate various features scale by scale during bio-inspired representation learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes a densely connected block to concatenate various features scale by scale. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The technical mechanism is stated the same way, but the publication lowers the certainty from high to medium, indicating a tempered confidence in the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed visual attention prediction method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in generating visual attention maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report experimental validation of effectiveness; the publication specifies 'in generating visual attention maps' but the meaning matches the preprint result.
